an4.37:1.1: “Endowed with four *dhammas*, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* is not liable to decline and is close to *nibbāna*.
an4.37:1.3: Here, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* is accomplished in ethical conduct, is guarded at the doors of the *indriyas*, knows moderation in eating, and is devoted to wakefulness.
an4.37:2.1: And how, *bhikkhus*, is a *bhikkhu* accomplished in ethical conduct?
an4.37:2.2: Here, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* is ethical; he dwells restrained by the restraint of the training code, accomplished in conduct and resort, seeing danger in the slightest faults; having undertaken them, he trains in the training rules.
an4.37:2.3: In this way, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* is accomplished in ethical conduct.
an4.37:3.1: And how, *bhikkhus*, is a *bhikkhu* guarded at the doors of the *indriyas*?
an4.37:3.10: In this way, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* is guarded at the doors of the *indriyas*.
an4.37:3.2: Here, *bhikkhus*, having seen form [*rūpaṁ*] with the eye, a *bhikkhu* is not one who grasps the *nimitta* and not one who grasps details.
an4.37:3.3: Since, if he were to dwell with the eye-*indriya* unrestrained, covetousness and distress, bad unskillful *dhammas*, might flow in upon him, he practices for its restraint; he guards the eye-*indriya*; he enters upon restraint in the eye-*indriya*.
an4.37:3.4: Having heard a sound with the ear …
an4.37:3.5: Having smelled an odor with the nose …
an4.37:3.6: Having tasted a flavor with the tongue …
an4.37:3.7: Having touched a tangible object with the body …
an4.37:3.8: Having known a *dhamma* with the mind, he is not one who grasps the *nimitta* and not one who grasps details.
an4.37:3.9: Since, if he were to dwell with the mind-*indriya* unrestrained, covetousness and distress, bad unskillful *dhammas*, might flow in upon him, he practices for its restraint; he guards the mind-*indriya*; he enters upon restraint in the mind-*indriya*.
an4.37:4.1: And how, *bhikkhus*, does a *bhikkhu* know moderation in eating?
an4.37:4.2: Here, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu*, reflecting appropriately, takes food:
an4.37:4.3: ‘not for amusement, not for intoxication, not for adornment, not for beautification; but only for the maintenance and continuance of this body, for the ending of harm, and for the support of the holy life. Thus I shall put an end to old *vedanā*, and I shall not produce new *vedanā*; and there will be for me sustenance, blamelessness, and a comfortable dwelling.’
an4.37:4.4: In this way, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* knows moderation in eating.
an4.37:5.1: And how, *bhikkhus*, is a *bhikkhu* devoted to wakefulness?
an4.37:5.2: Here, *bhikkhus*, during the day a *bhikkhu* purifies the mind [*cittaṁ*] from obstructive *dhammas* by walking meditation and sitting;
an4.37:5.3: in the first watch of the night, he purifies the mind [*cittaṁ*] from obstructive *dhammas* by walking meditation and sitting;
an4.37:5.4: in the middle watch of the night, he lies down on his right side in the lion’s posture, placing one foot on the other, with *sati*, fully aware [*sampajāno*], attending to the perception of rising;
an4.37:5.5: in the last watch of the night, after rising, he purifies the mind [*cittaṁ*] from obstructive *dhammas* by walking meditation and sitting.
an4.37:5.6: In this way, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* is devoted to wakefulness.
an4.37:5.7: Endowed with these four *dhammas*, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* is not liable to decline and is close to *nibbāna*.”
an4.37:7.4: for the attainment of security from bondage.
an4.37:8.2: or who sees danger in negligence;
